commit.message:
feat(agentserver-core): enrich spans with session ID and conversation ID from baggage (#46225)

* feat: enrich spans with session ID and conversation ID from baggage

Read azure.ai.agentserver.session_id and azure.ai.agentserver.conversation_id
from OTel baggage in on_start and stamp them as microsoft.session.id and
gen_ai.conversation.id respectively on all spans (root + framework children).

Session ID and conversation ID are independent — session_id never maps to
gen_ai.conversation.id. Also updated request_span() to map its session_id
param to microsoft.session.id instead of gen_ai.conversation.id.

commit.message:
Add ecosystem integration points to Appendix A

Add OpenRiaServices and external ecosystem projects that integrate with
DataAnnotations validation, documenting how each consumes the APIs:

- OpenRiaServices: direct Validator.TryValidateObject/Property consumer
  with recursive pipeline (.NET Foundation, targets .NET 8+)
- Blazor component vendors: MudBlazor, Radzen, Telerik, Syncfusion,
  DevExpress — all build on EditContext/DataAnnotationsValidator
- API frameworks: FastEndpoints (recursive TryValidateObject),
  MiniValidation (recursive graph walk with async support)
- Schema generators: Swashbuckle, NSwag (metadata consumers)
- Other: ServiceStack, FluentValidation

Updates mermaid diagram to show ecosystem tier.
Adds collaboration points section to the async support summary.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Implement arena-based region deallocation (Phase 1) (#65)

* Implement arena-based region deallocation (Phase 1)

Add SaveArenaState/RestoreArenaState IR instructions that snapshot and
restore the heap bump-allocator cursor at ownership scope boundaries.

When a scope's result is a copy type (Int, Float, Bool), RestoreArenaState
resets the heap cursor to the scope-entry watermark after Drop instructions
have run, effectively freeing all heap memory allocated within the scope.

Changes:
- Ir.cs: Add SaveArenaState and RestoreArenaState IR instruction records
- Lowering.cs: Emit SaveArenaState at PushOwnershipScope, conditionally
  emit RestoreArenaState at PopOwnershipScope for copy-type results
- LlvmCodegen.cs: Dispatch new instructions to backend codegen
- LlvmCodegenMemory.cs: Implement EmitSaveArenaState/EmitRestoreArenaState
- ArenaDeallocationTests.cs: 16 new tests covering IR emission, slot
  pairing, copy vs heap type behavior, nesting, and Drop interaction
- Update snapshot verified files for new IR shape
- Update COMPILER_OPTIMIZATION.md to document Phase 1 completion

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/MattiasHognas/Ashes/sessions/bb46feac-16f7-4c4e-b2ab-ad45abb143e0

Co-authored-by: MattiasHognas <1923344+MattiasHognas@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix arena watermark placement: emit SaveArenaState before let-bound value lowering

Decouples SaveArenaState emission from PushOwnershipScope into a separate
EmitArenaWatermark() method. In LowerLet, the watermark is now emitted
BEFORE lowering let.Value so heap allocations from the bound expression
(lists, tuples, ADTs) are covered by the arena scope.

Adds 3 tests verifying SaveArenaState precedes heap Alloc instructions
for list and tuple let-bound values, and that copy-type bodies still
emit RestoreArenaState to reclaim those allocations.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/MattiasHognas/Ashes/sessions/a6956db0-91c6-4e0a-90ee-52e971abc82f

Co-authored-by: MattiasHognas <1923344+MattiasHognas@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix match arm failure path: restore arena state on pattern/guard failure

Add per-arm cleanup label (Label → RestoreArenaState → Jump) so that when
a pattern or guard fails, the arena watermark is restored before jumping to
the next arm, preventing allocation accumulation from failed arms.

Update tests: strengthen cleanup path verification with structural assertions
(Label/Restore/Jump sequence), update string-result match test to account for
cleanup path RestoreArenaState.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/MattiasHognas/Ashes/sessions/019f3bce-6e64-4414-bf6d-fe93ef823084

Co-authored-by: MattiasHognas <1923344+MattiasHognas@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: MattiasHognas <1923344+MattiasHognas@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(eks): use certGen mode for VPA admission controller TLS

Remove the incorrectly-set registerWebhook: true value that conflicted
with the chart's default certGen.enabled: true, causing a hard template
error on every deploy.

The chart enforces that these two options are mutually exclusive:
- certGen mode (default): Helm hook jobs create the vpa-tls-certs
  Secret and patch the caBundle into the MutatingWebhookConfiguration.
  The admission controller starts with --register-webhook=false.
- registerWebhook mode: the admission controller manages the
  MutatingWebhookConfiguration itself, requiring cluster-wide delete
  permission on mutatingwebhookconfigurations — a privilege escalation
  risk flagged in the chart values docs.

The original comment claiming registerWebhook was required for webhook
registration was incorrect; certGen mode registers the webhook via Helm
hooks with no extra RBAC needed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add Chapter 13: Object Graph Validation

Inventory how each validation invocation model handles complex object
graphs with recursive walks and how validation results are represented:

- Core Validator.TryValidateObject: single-level only, no recursion
- ASP.NET Core MVC: full recursive graph walk via ValidationVisitor
  with cycle detection and configurable MaxValidationDepth
- Blazor: top-level only by default; experimental ObjectGraphDataAnnotationsValidator
- Options validation: opt-in recursion via [ValidateObjectMembers]
  and [ValidateEnumeratedItems] attributes
- Microsoft.Extensions.Validation (.NET 10): full recursive graph walk
- EF Core and OpenAPI: metadata only, no runtime validation

Ecosystem projects that fill the recursion gap:
- MiniValidation (Damian Edwards): recursive with cycle detection
- RecursiveDataAnnotationsValidation, DataAnnotationsValidatorRecursive
- FluentValidation: child validators and RuleForEach

Includes comparison table and implications for async validation design.
Updates nav links on ch12, appendix-a, and README TOC.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix race condition in label-triggered review workflows (#38093)

### Problem

When multiple review labels (e.g. `docs-review-needed` +
`test-review-needed`) are applied to a PR simultaneously, review
workflows silently fail to run.

**Root cause**: GitHub Actions `pull_request: types: [labeled]` fires
for *any* label — there is no way to filter by specific label at the
trigger level. So each label event triggers **all 7** review workflows.
The `if:` condition on each job correctly skips non-matching labels, but
the concurrency group (`gh-aw-<workflow>-<PR#>`) is evaluated first.

When the same workflow is triggered twice in quick succession (once per
label event), `cancel-in-progress: true` cancels the first run — which
may be the one whose label actually matched. The second run then skips
because its label does not match. Net result: the workflow never
completes.

**Example**: On
[#37993](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/pull/37993) at
`2026-04-10T15:32:11Z`, Documentation Review was cancelled by a
`test-review-needed` label event and never ran.

### Fix

Include `github.event.label.name` in the concurrency group for all 7
label-triggered review workflows.

- **Same workflow + same label** → same group → `cancel-in-progress`
works as intended
- **Same workflow + different label** → different groups → no
interference

### Why not make the trigger more specific?

GitHub Actions does not support filtering `labeled` events by label name
at the trigger level. Filtering can only happen via job-level `if:`
conditions, which execute after the concurrency group is resolved. The
concurrency group fix is the only viable approach.

### Affected workflows

`archie`, `dash`, `dexter`, `mgmt-review`, `scribe`, `sentinel`,
`tester`

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(starrocks): increase operator memory limits to prevent OOMKill

The operator was being OOMKilled during FE leader election reconciliation.
The previous 128Mi limit was insufficient for the reconciliation loop.

Raise the request to 256Mi so the operator is scheduled on a node with
adequate headroom, and raise the limit to 512Mi to give the reconciler
enough burst capacity without risking node-level memory pressure.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(capture): detect macOS screen recording permission and warn user (#66)

* fix(capture): detect macOS screen recording permission and warn user

- Extract enumerateAvfDevices() to share AVFoundation device enumeration
  between the permission check and device index lookup (ffmpeg runs once)
- Add checkScreenRecordingPermission() — returns { granted, deviceCount };
  darwin-only, non-darwin always returns granted: true
- ScreenCapture.start() throws a descriptive error on darwin when no
  Capture screen devices are found (permission denied)
- Proactively check permission on extension activation (macOS only) and
  show showErrorMessage with an 'Open System Settings' action button that
  deep-links to Privacy & Security → Screen Recording

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address three review comments on enumerateAvfDevices

- Replace cachedAvfDevices (string[] | null) with avfDeviceEnumeration
  (Promise<string[]> | null) so concurrent callers share the same
  in-flight ffmpeg invocation (fixes race condition)
- Reject with a clear error when execFile fails with ENOENT/EACCES and
  avoid caching the result, preventing a permission-denied misdiagnosis
  when ffmpeg itself is not executable (fixes _err ignored comment)
- Prefix the Screen Recording permission error thrown in
  ScreenCapture.start() with 'gEcho:' for consistency with all other
  user-facing errors in the module

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Add king travel system — royal progress and diplomatic visits

Kings can now embark on two types of trips:
- Royal Progress: tour own kingdom towns (up to 3), boosting happiness
  and prosperity in each visited town. Prioritizes troubled/large towns.
- Diplomatic Visit: travel to a foreign capital to improve relations.
  Weighted toward allies and friendly kingdoms. Blocked during war.

Travel decisions are personality+mood driven:
- Ambitious/brave/kind kings travel more often
- Paranoid/fearful/grieving kings never leave the capital
- Cowardly/lazy/content kings rarely travel
- Wrathful kings mostly stay home

Safety rules ensure king is home when needed:
- Returns immediately when war is declared (diplomatic trips)
- Returns for royal tournaments
- Won't travel during succession crisis or low treasury (<2000g)
- 60-day cooldown between trips (king is home ~75%+ of the time)
- Daily gold cost for travel (5g/day progress, 10g/day diplomatic)

Config: CONFIG.KING_TRAVEL with all tunable parameters.
UI: Travel status shown in kingdom detail panel and world overview.
Help: New 'King Travel' entry in help system.
Save compat: kingTravel and _lastTravelEndDay added to load defaults.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(starrocks): encrypt shared-data bucket with data-analytics KMS key

The shared-data S3 bucket was created with default SSE-S3 encryption.
Switch to the same KMS key used by all other lakehouse buckets
(kms_s3_data_analytics_key from the KMS stack) with bucket-key enabled
to reduce per-request KMS call overhead.

Also extend the IRSA inline policy with kms:GenerateDataKey and
kms:Decrypt on the key ARN so FE/CN pods can read and write encrypted
objects without hitting AccessDenied from KMS.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update Mahant Swami Maharaj start date

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix --update: track source clone path for git pull + re-copy

Installer now saves the git clone path to .source-path in the module
directory. --update reads this to git pull in the original clone, then
re-copies all module files and reinstalls the Python MCP package.

Previously --update checked for .git in the module dir (always a copy,
never a repo) so it always failed with 'not a git-based install'.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(starrocks): create shared-data S3 bucket and wire fe.conf for CN mode

CN (shared-storage) mode requires an S3 bucket for persistent cluster state:
tablet data, metadata snapshots, and compaction output.  Without it the FE
reports no available capacity and all queries fail.

Changes:
- Create OLBucket named ol-starrocks-<env_prefix>-<env_suffix> when use_cn=true
  (e.g. ol-starrocks-lakehouse-qa, ol-starrocks-lakehouse-production)
- Attach inline IAM policy to the IRSA role granting s3:GetObject/PutObject/
  DeleteObject on the bucket objects and s3:ListBucket/GetBucketLocation on
  the bucket itself
- Unify fe.conf building into _build_fe_config() which composes SSL and
  shared-data sections independently; extends the chart-version guard to also
  cover the CN path since both sections rely on the same base config
- fe.conf shared-data additions (all static params requiring FE restart):
    run_mode = shared_data
    cloud_native_meta_port = 6090
    cloud_native_storage_type = S3
    aws_s3_path = <bucket>
    aws_s3_region = <region>
    aws_s3_endpoint = https://s3.<region>.amazonaws.com
    aws_s3_use_instance_profile = true   (covers both EC2 profile and IRSA)
    enable_load_volume_from_conf = true  (must be explicit; default=false in 3.4.1+)
- Export shared_data_bucket_name stack output for downstream reference
- Hoist aws_region read to module scope (was duplicated inside enable_data_lake block)
- Add json import (used by inline IAM policy)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: fall back to live GitHub API when star history DB is empty

Projects synced before the StarHistory feature was added have no
data in the table. Now the stars API route fetches live from
GitHub and persists the results when the DB is empty.
